parts.name: Core Glow
parts.focus: Bright nucleus and dense central light concentration.
parts.details: Use this region to validate the default selected state and metadata layout against the brightest part of the image.

parts.name: Dust Lanes
parts.focus: Dark diagonal banding that cuts across the galactic disk.
parts.details: A useful target for future box or polygon regions once the app moves beyond single-point hotspots.

parts.name: Outer Halo
parts.focus: Fainter edge structure beyond the brightest disk.
parts.details: This gives the UI a lower-contrast region that still feels connected to the same image narrative.

parts.name: Pillar Region
parts.focus: The broader area containing the famous Pillars of Creation.
parts.details: The WISE view is wider and softer than Hubble or Webb, so this region is a good example of browsing context before detail.

parts.name: Inner Ridge
parts.focus: A brighter transition where warm tones cluster near the nebula's center.
parts.details: This is a practical stand-in for future annotations tied to notable structures or reference notes.

parts.name: Outer Field
parts.focus: Surrounding nebulosity that shows the wider context around the pillars.
parts.details: The broader field helps test image navigation without forcing every region to be tightly zoomed.

parts.name: Hidden Core
parts.focus: Brighter central nebula emerging from the infrared color field.
parts.details: This is the region most likely to become the default focus card when we add deep links or saved selections.

parts.name: Dust Veil
parts.focus: Dark obscuring material framing the brighter nebular interior.
parts.details: A useful target for any future overlay mode that needs to emphasize boundaries rather than single points.

parts.name: Outer Clouds
parts.focus: Lower-contrast outer region where the nebula fades into the frame.
parts.details: This helps test whether the right-side metadata remains useful even when the region itself is visually subtle.
